name: Direct kinematic upper limit on the electron-neutrino mass (KATRIN)
requirement: Any candidate framework must predict an effective electron-neutrino mass m_nu_e below the KATRIN upper limit. KATRIN uses precision tritium beta-decay endpoint spectroscopy to set m_nu_e < 0.45 eV/c^2 at 90% CL (2024 update), the most stringent direct kinematic bound. Final KATRIN sensitivity goal is ~0.2 eV.

regime.description: Tritium beta-decay endpoint at 18.6 keV; sensitivity to the 'effective' electron-neutrino mass m_beta = sqrt(sum_i |U_ei|^2 m_i^2).

current_bound.units: eV/c^2 (90% CL upper limit on m_nu_e)
current_bound.reference: Aker et al. (KATRIN Collaboration), 'Direct neutrino-mass measurement based on 259 days of KATRIN data', arXiv:2406.13516 (2024); preceded by Aker et al., Nature Physics 18, 160 (2022), arXiv:2105.08533

what_it_excludes: Independent of cosmological assumptions. Constrains models in which neutrino masses are O(eV) (sterile-neutrino dark matter at the eV scale, certain neutrino-philic Higgs models). Combined with the cosmological Sum m_nu < 0.072 eV bound (DESI), forces the lightest neutrino to be quasi-degenerate at sub-eV with the heavier flavors.

parameterization.name: Effective electron-neutrino mass m_beta = (sum_i |U_ei|^2 m_i^2)^(1/2), where U is the PMNS matrix and m_i are the mass eigenstate masses.
parameterization.reference: Drexlin, Hannen, Mertens, Weinheimer, Adv. High Energy Phys. 2013, 293986 (2013), arXiv:1307.0101.
parameterization.summary: Tritium beta spectrum near the endpoint depends on m_beta through a kinematic phase-space factor; KATRIN fits the endpoint to extract m_beta^2.

procedural.predicted_observable: Effective beta-decay neutrino mass m_beta, related to mass eigenstates via mixing.
framework_inputs: If the framework predicts non-standard neutrino mass spectrum (sterile, decaying, or modified flavor structure), compute m_beta directly from the framework's mass and mixing matrices.

primary_references.citation: Aker et al. (KATRIN Collaboration), 'Direct neutrino-mass measurement based on 259 days of KATRIN data' (2024)
Aker et al. (KATRIN Collaboration), 'Direct neutrino-mass measurement with sub-electronvolt sensitivity', Nature Physics 18, 160 (2022)
